channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Centre clarifies no plan to raise petrol or diesel prices despite global oil surge
item.description: The Union Government on Thursday clarified that there is no proposal under consideration to raise petrol and diesel prices, even as a report by Kotak Institutional Equities suggested a possible increa...

item.title: Pakistan Urges UNSC to Address India's Suspension of Indus Waters Treaty
item.description: Pakistan has raised alarm over the consequences of India’s unilateral suspension of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT), urging the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) to intervene. In a letter to the UN...

item.title: Warner Bros shareholders back Paramount deal amid regulatory and industry concerns
item.description: Shareholders of Warner Bros. Discovery have overwhelmingly approved a proposed $US110 billion ($151 billion) merger with Paramount Skydance, marking a major step towards creating one of the largest me...

item.title: High Court Halts TVETA from Closing KIM Campuses Pending Legal Challenge
item.description: The Kenya Institute of Management (KIM) has successfully obtained a court injunction stopping the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) from closing down all its campuses a...

item.title: Commission report outlines five underlying causes of October 29 election violence in Tanzania
item.description: Tanzania’s Commission of Inquiry into the post–October 29, 2025 election violence has released its final report, identifying five major underlying causes of the unrest. The report was presented on Apr...

item.title: What’s next for Duterte after ICC confirms charges over drug war killings?
item.description: The International Criminal Court (ICC) has confirmed the charges of crimes against humanity against former President Rodrigo Duterte, stemming from the killings during his war on illegal drugs. The IC...

item.title: ASI Begins Sand Removal for Conservation at Konark Sun Temple's Jagamohan After Over a Century
item.description: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has initiated the sand extraction process at the Jagamohan (assembly hall) of the Konark Sun Temple in Odisha, a crucial step in stabilizing and conserving thi...

item.title: Daily Superquiz and Mini Crossword Offering for April 24
item.description: This short piece from The Age promotes its daily puzzle offerings, focusing on the Superquiz for Friday, April 24, alongside the publication’s Mini Crossword feature. Rather than presenting traditiona...

item.title: Catholic Leaders and Communities Respond to JD Vance's Remarks on Pope Leo XIV and Just War
item.description: During a recent Turning Point USA event in Georgia, Vice President JD Vance, a Catholic convert, made remarks criticizing Pope Leo XIV’s statements on war and theology. Vance warned the pope to be car...

item.title: Sindh Government Declares May 1 as Public Holiday for Labour Day
item.description: The Sindh government has announced that May 1, 2026 (Friday), will be a public holiday in the province in observance of Labour Day. A notification issued today confirms that all government offices, se...

item.title: Suspension of Goods Transport Causes Food and Fuel Shortages in Rawalpindi and Islamabad
item.description: Rawalpindi and Islamabad are currently facing shortages of essential food items and fuel due to strict security measures that have suspended the movement of goods transport. Traders and transport asso...

item.title: Fox News Hosts Discuss Republican-Led Gerrymandering Efforts
item.description: On the Fox News show 'The Five,' liberal co-host Jessica Tarlov acknowledged that Jesse Watters, her conservative counterpart, made a correct observation regarding Republican-led gerrymandering effort...

item.title: Co-op Bank Plans Transition to Holding Company Structure Pending Shareholder Approval
item.description: Co-operative Bank of Kenya has announced that it will hold its Annual General Meeting (AGM) on May 15 to seek approval from shareholders for a major corporate restructuring. The proposed plan will see...

item.title: US Navy Secretary John Phelan Removed Amid Pentagon Leadership Shakeup
item.description: The US Navy Secretary, John Phelan, has been dismissed from his post, confirmed by sources familiar with the matter, marking another significant leadership change at the Pentagon during ongoing milita...

item.title: DSS Arraigns El-Rufai Over Alleged Illegal Access to NSA Phone Conversation
item.description: The Department of State Services (DSS) don carry former Kaduna State Governor, Nasir El-Rufai, go Federal High Court for Abuja over allegation say he illegally access one phone conversation wey involv...

item.title: Lebanese Journalist Killed in Israeli Airstrike Near Israel-Lebanon Border
item.description: A Lebanese journalist, Amal Khalil, was killed in an Israeli airstrike on April 22, 2026, while working near the Israeli-Lebanese border. Khalil, a veteran correspondent for Al-Akhbar, was in the town...

item.title: Exploitation of women in international cyber‑scam networks affecting Australians
item.description: A recent report by the Australian Institute of Criminology has uncovered the disturbing exploitation of women in international cyber‑scam operations that target Australians. Victims, often young women...

item.title: Pakistan plans major power expansion to meet future demand and reduce reliance on imported fuels by 2035
item.description: Pakistan’s government has projected that the country will require between 62,660MW and 70,720MW of additional power generation capacity by 2035 to sustain economic growth ranging from around 3.5% to 6...

item.title: Canada Post to maintain home mail delivery for rural addresses
item.description: Canada Post has confirmed that residents in rural areas with individual roadside mailboxes will continue to receive home mail delivery for the foreseeable future. This announcement comes as the Crown ...

item.title: Why Studying History Matters: Understanding the Past to Comprehend the Present
item.description: The article explores the question of why studying history is valuable, arguing against the notion that knowledge should only be pursued for practical utility or social advancement. The author critique...
